subject: LSJ
body:
I just got back from the closing to find a fax from John Amerling of a  
letter John says that he received by certified mail today, even though the  letter 
is dated January 15.  I had Rhanna e-mail a copy of the letter to  you.
 
Although the letter seems to state that all of the modifications that we  
have filed for over the past couple of years are having an adverse impact on the  
Island, the letter also seems to state that the result of this adverse impact 
is  that the November 2007 application for permit modification must be 
submitted and  reviewed as a major permit.  The letter does not seem to address any 
of the  permits and modifications that have already been granted.
 
According to John, the November 2007 application is for a modification of  
the existing compound permit granted on Janary 27, 2007.  The modification  
decreases the footprint of the permitted work on the compound, yet it is  now 
going to requre a major permit. 
 
John has no explanation regarding the actual date of the letter being  
January 15.
 
Let's discuss when you are able.
 
Sincerely,

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison  Avenue
Fourth Floor
New York, New York 10022
Telephone:  212-750-1176
Telecopier: 212-750-0381
Mobile: 862-485-6315
e-mail:  dkiesq@aol.com


------------------------------

federal, state, or local  tax penalties. Unless otherwise specifically indicated

federal, state, or local  tax matter was written in connection with the 
promotion or marketing by other  parties of the transaction(s) or matter(s) addressed 
in this email. Each  taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's 
particular circumstances  from an independent tax advisor.

body_html:
I just got back from the closing to find a fax from John Amerling of a letter John says that he received by certified mail today, even though the letter is dated January 15.  I had Rhanna e-mail a copy of the letter to you.
 
Although the letter seems to state that all of the modifications that we have filed for over the past couple of years are having an adverse impact on the Island, the letter also seems to state that the result of this adverse impact is that the November 2007 application for permit modification must be submitted and reviewed as a major permit.  The letter does not seem to address any of the permits and modifications that have already been granted.
 
According to John, the November 2007 application is for a modification of the existing compound permit granted on Janary 27, 2007.  The modification decreases the footprint of the permitted work on the compound, yet it is now going to requre a major permit. 
 
John has no explanation regarding the actual date of the letter being January 15.
 
Let's discuss when you are able.
 
Sincerely,

Darren K. Indyke
Attorney-At-Law
457 Madison Avenue
Fourth Floor
New York, New York 10022
Telephone: 212-750-1176
Telecopier: 212-750-0381
Mobile: 862-485-6315
e-mail: dkiesq@aol.com

Each taxpayer should seek advice based on the taxpayer's particular circumstances from an independent tax advisor.
